{{Quote|He was a powerful student. Even after he left, he was regarded with respect.|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]|<ref name="RP" />}}
'''Leor Hal''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Golden Age of the Old Republic|Golden Age]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], Leor Hal was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at its [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. During his training as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], Hal developed the [[Potentium]] and established a sizable following of fellow apprentices. Having formed a cult in the eyes of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], Hal's followers were cast out from the Temple circa [[132 BBY/Legends|132 BBY]], causing him to renounce his training in protest and leave as well.<ref name="JVS" />

Without his followers, Hal wandered through space for years until he eventually found the living [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]]. In [[89 BBY]], he encouraged groups of [[Ferroan]] and [[Langhesi]] colonists to settle on the planet, and he made himself the first [[Magister]] of the planet. During his time there, Hal discovered the being [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Sekot]], with whom Hal formed a symbiotic relationship with and used this to help transform the planet into a thriving manufacturing organ. Having imprinted his own philosophy of the Force on Sekot and the settlers, Hal began the [[Zonama Sekot Shipbuilders]] and the planet began manufacturing [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] beneath the planet's surface as well as the rare [[Zonama Sekotan fighter|living ships]] the planet was famed for.<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

Following his death, the citizens of Zonama banned speaking the name of their leader in reverence to his great deeds. Taking up the reigns of governance, Hal's [[Unidentified Magister|son]] led the planet from his distant [[Magister's Palace|palace]] for sometime until the arrival of the [[Yuuzhan Vong|Far Outsiders]]. Unbeknownst to the Zonaman people, the Magister and his palace were destroyed, with Sekot crafting a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] to report to his people and maintain the calm of the populace.<ref name="RP" />
